message:
feat(ensemble): add ModeRef, CacheConfig, AgentDistribution types and extend EnsemblePreset

Add new configuration types for the ensemble system (bd-35e81):
- ModeRef: references modes by ID or taxonomy code, canonicalizes at load time
- CacheConfig: context pack cache control with TTL, max entries, share-across-modes
- AgentDistribution: configures mode-to-agent mapping strategy
- Extend EnsemblePreset with DisplayName, Synthesis, Budget, Cache, AllowAdvanced,
  AgentDistribution fields, reusing existing BudgetConfig and SynthesisConfig
- Add YAML struct tags alongside existing JSON/TOML tags
- Add Ensemble struct fields: Cache, AllowAdvanced, AgentDistribution
- Comprehensive tests: resolution, serialization roundtrip, zero-value, duplicates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,1108 @@
+package ensemble
+
+// EmbeddedModes contains the complete 80-mode reasoning taxonomy.
+// Core-tier modes (~28) are the curated default set for UX.
+// Advanced-tier modes (remaining) are accessible via --all or --tier flags.
+//
+// Mode codes follow the A-L category mapping:
+//
+//	A = Formal, B = Ampliative, C = Uncertainty, D = Vagueness,
+//	E = Change, F = Causal, G = Practical, H = Strategic,
+//	I = Dialectical, J = Modal, K = Domain, L = Meta
+var EmbeddedModes = []ReasoningMode{
+	// =========================================================================
+	// A) Formal and mathematical reasoning (A1-A8)
+	// =========================================================================
+	{
+		ID:             "deductive",
+		Code:           "A1",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Deductive Inference",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Classical logical inference from premises to conclusions",
+		Description:    "If the premises are true and the inference rules are valid, the conclusion must be true. Truth-preserving and typically monotonic.",
+		Outputs:        "Valid entailments; proofs/derivations; contradictions/counterexamples",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Spec checking", "Compliance logic", "Formal arguments", "Must/shall implications"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Garbage-in (false premises)", "Missing premises that matter in reality"},
+		Differentiator: "Truth-preserving: makes explicit what is already implicit in premises",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "mathematical-proof",
+		Code:           "A2",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Mathematical Proof",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Proof-theoretic reasoning with formal derivations",
+		Description:    "Deduction where the proof object matters—what counts as a proof, how it is constructed. Emphasizes derivability and proof structure.",
+		Outputs:        "Formal proofs (sometimes machine-checkable); proof transformations",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Formal methods", "Theorem proving", "Certified reasoning pipelines"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Proving the wrong theorem (spec mismatch)", "Proving something irrelevant to outcomes"},
+		Differentiator: "More structured than everyday deduction; emphasizes derivability and proof structure",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "formal-verification",
+		Code:           "A3",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Formal Verification",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Constructive proofs that correspond to verified programs",
+		Description:    "A proof of existence must provide a construction/witness. Proofs correspond to algorithms (proofs as programs). Stronger link between proved and computable.",
+		Outputs:        "Proofs that correspond to algorithms; verified implementations",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Verified software", "Protocol verification", "Constructive math", "Show me the witness"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Over-constraining when classical reasoning is acceptable and simpler"},
+		Differentiator: "Ensures proofs are constructive and correspond to executable programs",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "equational",
+		Code:           "A4",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Equational Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Transform expressions using equalities and rewrite rules",
+		Description:    "Transform expressions using equalities and rewrite rules while preserving meaning. Deduction specialized to symbol manipulation.",
+		Outputs:        "Equivalent forms; normal forms; simplifications; invariants",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Refactoring", "Optimization proofs", "Dimensional reasoning", "Invariant manipulation"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Unsound rewrite rules", "Implicit domain restrictions (division by zero, overflow)"},
+		Differentiator: "Deduction specialized to symbol manipulation; the everyday workhorse in math/CS",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "model-theoretic",
+		Code:           "A5",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Model-Theoretic Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Reason by constructing models that satisfy a theory",
+		Description:    "Reason by constructing/analyzing models that satisfy a theory. Instead of derive, build a world where it holds or does not.",
+		Outputs:        "Satisfiable/unsatisfiable; countermodels; interpretations",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Consistency checks", "Finding hidden assumptions", "Generating counterexamples"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Model doesn't match the intended semantics of the real system"},
+		Differentiator: "Complements proof-theory: builds worlds rather than deriving conclusions",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "constraint-sat",
+		Code:           "A6",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Constraint Satisfaction",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Encode requirements as constraints and solve for assignments",
+		Description:    "Encode requirements as constraints and solve for assignments that satisfy them (or prove none exist). Mode is solve-by-consistency.",
+		Outputs:        "Satisfying assignments; unsat certificates; minimal unsat cores; counterexamples",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Scheduling", "Configuration", "Verification", "Policy enforcement", "Feasibility checks"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Poor encoding (missed constraints) leading to false confidence"},
+		Differentiator: "Solve-by-consistency rather than argument-by-argument inference",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "type-theoretic",
+		Code:           "A7",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Type-Theoretic Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Use types to enforce invariants and compositional guarantees",
+		Description:    "Use types (including dependent/refinement types) to enforce invariants. Propositions as types in some systems. Reasoning integrated into construction.",
+		Outputs:        "Type derivations; well-typed programs; compositional guarantees",
+		BestFor:        []string{"API design", "Correctness-by-construction", "Safe composition of large systems"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Fighting the type system instead of clarifying the spec it encodes"},
+		Differentiator: "Reasoning is integrated into construction; great for modular correctness",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "edge-case",
+		Code:           "A8",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Edge Case Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryFormal,
+		ShortDesc:      "Find counterexamples by iterative abstraction refinement",
+		Description:    "Propose an abstraction, check it, if a counterexample appears refine the abstraction and repeat. A loop blending deduction + model checking + refinement.",
+		Outputs:        "Either a proof of property or a concrete counterexample; refined models",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Verification", "Security properties", "Systems where full modeling is too expensive"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Endless refinement loops if the abstraction boundary is poorly chosen"},
+		Differentiator: "Iterative loop that scales verification through progressive refinement",
+	},
+
+	// =========================================================================
+	// B) Ampliative reasoning (B1-B11)
+	// =========================================================================
+	{
+		ID:             "inductive",
+		Code:           "B1",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Inductive Generalization",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Infer general patterns from observations",
+		Description:    "Infer general patterns from observations. Not truth-preserving; new data can overturn conclusions.",
+		Outputs:        "General rules; trends; predictors",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Learning from experience", "Pattern discovery", "Forming priors"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Overgeneralizing from small/biased samples"},
+		Differentiator: "Goes beyond premises to form general rules; the basis of empirical learning",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "statistical",
+		Code:           "B2",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Statistical Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Frequentist inference from samples via estimators and tests",
+		Description:    "Inference about populations from samples via estimators, confidence intervals, tests, error rates. Emphasizes long-run properties of procedures.",
+		Outputs:        "Effect estimates + uncertainty tied to sampling procedures",
+		BestFor:        []string{"A/B tests", "QA", "Inference under repeated-sampling assumptions"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"P-value worship", "Confusing no evidence with evidence of no effect"},
+		Differentiator: "Avoids probability of hypotheses; emphasizes long-run procedure properties",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "bayesian",
+		Code:           "B3",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Bayesian Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Update degrees of belief with evidence via Bayes' rule",
+		Description:    "Represent degrees of belief as probabilities and update them with evidence. Probability as rational credence management.",
+		Outputs:        "Posterior beliefs; predictive distributions; uncertainty-aware forecasts",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Integrating prior knowledge + data", "Diagnosis", "Forecasting", "Online learning"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Overconfident priors", "Making up priors without sensitivity analysis"},
+		Differentiator: "Probability as rational credence management; coherence through Bayes' rule",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "likelihood",
+		Code:           "B4",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Likelihood Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Compare hypotheses via how well they predict observed data",
+		Description:    "Compare how well hypotheses predict observed data via likelihoods without necessarily committing to priors.",
+		Outputs:        "Likelihood ratios; relative evidential support rankings",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Model comparison", "Forensic evidence strength", "Hypothesis triage"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Ignoring base rates/priors entirely when they matter for decisions"},
+		Differentiator: "Separates data support from belief after priors; sits between Bayesian and frequentist",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "option-generation",
+		Code:           "B5",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Option Generation",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Abductive inference to generate best candidate explanations",
+		Description:    "From observations, propose hypotheses that would best explain them. Introduces hidden mechanisms and causes. Not guaranteed but creative.",
+		Outputs:        "Candidate explanations/models; best current story; generated options",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Hypothesis generation", "Incident triage", "Diagnosis", "Discovery"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Story bias (choosing most appealing explanation, not most supported)"},
+		Differentiator: "Introduces hidden mechanisms/causes; creative generation of possibilities",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "analogical",
+		Code:           "B6",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Analogical Transfer",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Transfer relational structure from known to new domains",
+		Description:    "Transfer relational structure from a known domain/case to a new one, often deeper than surface similarity.",
+		Outputs:        "Candidate inferences; adapted solutions; conceptual models/metaphors",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Innovation", "Design", "Teaching", "Cross-domain problem solving"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"False analogies (shared surface traits, different causal structure)"},
+		Differentiator: "Particular-to-particular transfer; seeds abduction with structural parallels",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "case-based",
+		Code:           "B7",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Case-Based Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Retrieve similar past cases and adapt their solutions",
+		Description:    "Retrieve similar past cases and adapt their solutions. Emphasizes retrieval metrics + adaptation operators + case libraries.",
+		Outputs:        "Proposed solutions justified by precedent; playbook actions",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Precedent reasoning", "Customer support", "Clinical decision support", "Ops playbooks"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Cargo-culting: applying precedent without checking context changes"},
+		Differentiator: "More operational than analogy: emphasizes retrieval and adaptation mechanics",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "conceptual-blending",
+		Code:           "B8",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Conceptual Blending",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Combine explanations into generalized reusable strategies",
+		Description:    "Use explanations of why solutions work to generalize reusable rules and plans. Generalizes like induction but guided by deductive explanation.",
+		Outputs:        "Generalized strategies with explanatory justification; novel concept combinations",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Turning expert solutions into SOPs", "Reducing overfitting to anecdotes", "Creative synthesis"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Explanations that are internally elegant but empirically wrong"},
+		Differentiator: "Induction guided and validated by deductive explanation; creativity through combination",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "simplicity",
+		Code:           "B9",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Simplicity Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Prefer hypotheses with fewer assumptions (Occam, MDL)",
+		Description:    "Prefer hypotheses that explain data with fewer assumptions or shorter descriptions, balancing fit vs complexity.",
+		Outputs:        "Bias toward simpler models; complexity penalties; regularization choices",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Model selection", "Avoiding overfitting", "Choosing parsimonious policies"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Oversimplifying when the world is genuinely complex/nonlinear"},
+		Differentiator: "Selection principle across hypotheses; often paired with abduction and statistics",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "reference-class",
+		Code:           "B10",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Reference-Class Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Predict by comparing to base rates of similar past cases",
+		Description:    "Predict by comparing to a base rate distribution of similar past projects/cases. Designed to counter planning fallacy and inside-view optimism.",
+		Outputs:        "Base-rate forecasts; adjustment factors",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Project timelines", "Budgets", "Risk forecasting", "Portfolio-level planning"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Choosing the wrong reference class (too broad or too narrow)"},
+		Differentiator: "Inductive method specifically designed to counter planning fallacy",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "fermi",
+		Code:           "B11",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Fermi Estimation",
+		Category:       CategoryAmpliative,
+		ShortDesc:      "Rough quantitative estimates via decomposition and bounding",
+		Description:    "Rough quantitative estimates via decomposition and bounding. Aims for scale correctness rather than precision.",
+		Outputs:        "Back-of-the-envelope estimates; upper/lower bounds; sensitivity drivers",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Early feasibility", "Sanity checks", "Identifying dominant terms"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Hidden unit mistakes or implicit assumptions left untested"},
+		Differentiator: "Heuristic quantitative mode aiming for scale correctness, not precision",
+	},
+
+	// =========================================================================
+	// C) Reasoning under uncertainty (C1-C5)
+	// =========================================================================
+	{
+		ID:             "probabilistic-logic",
+		Code:           "C1",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Probabilistic Logic",
+		Category:       CategoryUncertainty,
+		ShortDesc:      "Blend logical structure with probabilistic uncertainty",
+		Description:    "Blend logical structure (relations/rules/quantifiers) with probabilistic uncertainty. More expressive than plain Bayesian models for relational domains.",
+		Outputs:        "Probabilistic inferences over structured worlds; uncertain rule consequences",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Knowledge graphs with uncertainty", "Uncertain policies", "Relational prediction"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Model soup: too expressive leading to hard validation and brittle inference"},
+		Differentiator: "More expressive than plain Bayesian for relational domains",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "imprecise-probability",
+		Code:           "C2",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Imprecise Probability",
+		Category:       CategoryUncertainty,
+		ShortDesc:      "Represent uncertainty with probability ranges when precision isn't justified",
+		Description:    "Represent uncertainty with ranges of probabilities when precision is not justified. Separates unknown from unlikely.",
+		Outputs:        "Bounds on beliefs and decisions; sensitivity analyses",
+		BestFor:        []string{"High-stakes decisions with weak priors", "Governance/risk", "Robustness checks"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Paralysis (ranges are wide so we can't decide) without decision rules"},
+		Differentiator: "Less committal than a single prior/posterior; separates unknown from unlikely",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "evidential",
+		Code:           "C3",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Evidential Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryUncertainty,
+		ShortDesc:      "Allocate belief mass to sets of possibilities (Dempster-Shafer)",
+		Description:    "Allocate mass to sets of possibilities; combine evidence into belief/plausibility intervals.",
+		Outputs:        "Belief + plausibility ranges; fused evidence from multiple sources",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Multi-source fusion", "Ambiguous evidence", "Partial identification"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Misusing combination rules when sources are not independent"},
+		Differentiator: "Can represent partial support for sets more directly than standard probability",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "maximum-entropy",
+		Code:           "C4",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Maximum-Entropy Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryUncertainty,
+		ShortDesc:      "Choose least-committal distributions satisfying known constraints",
+		Description:    "Choose distributions satisfying known constraints while assuming as little else as possible. Least-committal completion.",
+		Outputs:        "Principled default distributions; minimally committed priors under constraints",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Baselines", "Priors under constraints", "Principled defaults in modeling"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Constraints are wrong/underspecified; outputs look objective but are not"},
+		Differentiator: "Least-committal completion rather than explanation",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "qualitative-probability",
+		Code:           "C5",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Qualitative Probability",
+		Category:       CategoryUncertainty,
+		ShortDesc:      "Ordinal plausibility ranks instead of numeric probabilities",
+		Description:    "Replace numeric probabilities with ordinal degree-of-disbelief ranks. Update by shifting ranks. Useful when only ordering is defensible.",
+		Outputs:        "Ordered plausibility levels; belief dynamics without precise probabilities",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Early-stage hypothesis ranking", "Reasoning with weak quantification"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Losing important magnitude information when magnitude actually matters"},
+		Differentiator: "More structured than defaults, less numeric than Bayes; ordering without magnitudes",
+	},
+
+	// =========================================================================
+	// D) Reasoning under vagueness (D1-D5)
+	// =========================================================================
+	{
+		ID:             "fuzzy",
+		Code:           "D1",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Fuzzy Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryVagueness,
+		ShortDesc:      "Truth as a degree for predicates with blurred boundaries",
+		Description:    "Truth is a degree (0-1) because predicates have blurred boundaries. Fuzzy truth is not probability: it is graded applicability.",
+		Outputs:        "Degrees of membership/truth; fuzzy rule outputs",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Control systems", "Scoring/rubrics", "Policies with soft thresholds"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Treating fuzzy scores like calibrated probabilities"},
+		Differentiator: "Fuzzy membership is graded applicability, not uncertainty about crisp facts",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "ambiguity-detection",
+		Code:           "D2",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Ambiguity Detection",
+		Category:       CategoryVagueness,
+		ShortDesc:      "Identify unknown, undefined, and indeterminate states",
+		Description:    "More than two truth values; explicitly represent unknown or undefined. Inferences that track indeterminacy rather than forcing a binary choice.",
+		Outputs:        "Inferences tracking indeterminacy; identified ambiguities; clarification needs",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Databases with nulls", "Partial specs", "Missingness-aware reasoning"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Conflating unknown with false"},
+		Differentiator: "Targets incompleteness and ambiguity; surfaces what is truly unknown",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "rough-set",
+		Code:           "D3",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Rough Set Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryVagueness,
+		ShortDesc:      "Approximate concepts by definite-in vs possibly-in given features",
+		Description:    "Approximate a concept by what is definitely in vs possibly in, given limited features/indiscernibility.",
+		Outputs:        "Lower/upper bounds on classifications; boundary regions",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Interpretability-focused classification", "Feature-limited domains"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Overconfidence about what is definitely in/out when features are weak"},
+		Differentiator: "Membership arises from granularity of observation, not degrees of truth",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "prototype-reasoning",
+		Code:           "D4",
+		Tier:           TierCore,
+		Name:           "Prototype Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryVagueness,
+		ShortDesc:      "Categorize by similarity to prototypes rather than strict definitions",
+		Description:    "Categorize by similarity to prototypes/exemplars rather than strict necessary-and-sufficient definitions. Graded category judgments.",
+		Outputs:        "Graded category judgments; typicality effects",
+		BestFor:        []string{"UX taxonomies", "Product categorization", "Human-facing labeling"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Hidden bias in prototypes", "Category drift over time"},
+		Differentiator: "Natural for human categories; complements fuzzy/rough with similarity geometry",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "qualitative",
+		Code:           "D5",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Qualitative Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryVagueness,
+		ShortDesc:      "Reason with signs and monotone influences, not exact numbers",
+		Description:    "Reason with qualitative states (increasing, decreasing, positive influence) instead of exact numbers. Coarse modeling for robustness.",
+		Outputs:        "Directional predictions; qualitative constraints; sanity checks",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Early architecture", "Feedback reasoning", "Does this trend make sense checks"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Missing nonlinear thresholds where sign reasoning breaks down"},
+		Differentiator: "Not primarily uncertainty; coarse modeling for robustness and early design",
+	},
+
+	// =========================================================================
+	// E) Reasoning with inconsistency, defaults, and change (E1-E7)
+	// =========================================================================
+	{
+		ID:             "non-monotonic",
+		Code:           "E1",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Non-Monotonic Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryChange,
+		ShortDesc:      "Adding information can retract previous conclusions",
+		Description:    "Adding information can retract previous conclusions. Most real knowledge bases are non-monotonic unlike classical deduction.",
+		Outputs:        "Default conclusions with explicit revision behavior",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Rule systems with exceptions", "Policies", "Normally-qualified knowledge"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Unclear priority rules leading to inconsistent or surprising behavior"},
+		Differentiator: "Classical deduction is monotonic; most real knowledge is not",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "default-typicality",
+		Code:           "E2",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Default Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryChange,
+		ShortDesc:      "Use normally/typically rules overridden by more specific info",
+		Description:    "Use normally/typically rules overridden by more specific info. Often categorical rather than numeric probabilities.",
+		Outputs:        "Typical conclusions; exception handling",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Ontologies", "Rule engines", "SOPs with carve-outs"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Defaults become facts and stop being questioned"},
+		Differentiator: "Categorical (default applies or not) rather than numeric probabilities",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "defeasible",
+		Code:           "E3",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Defeasible Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryChange,
+		ShortDesc:      "Tentative conclusions that can be defeated by counterevidence",
+		Description:    "Conclusions can be defeated by counterevidence or stronger rules. Tracks priorities and strength of support.",
+		Outputs:        "Warranted conclusions given competing reasons",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Compliance/policy", "Medical guidelines", "Conflicting requirements"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Priority schemes that encode politics rather than relevance"},
+		Differentiator: "More explicit about conflict resolution than plain defaults",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "belief-revision",
+		Code:           "E4",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Belief Revision",
+		Category:       CategoryChange,
+		ShortDesc:      "Revise accepted belief sets with new info (AGM-style)",
+		Description:    "Principles for revising an accepted belief set with new info, especially when inconsistent. Minimal-change goals.",
+		Outputs:        "Revised belief sets with minimal-change goals",
+		BestFor:        []string{"Knowledge management", "Requirements evolution", "Source reconciliation"},
+		FailureModes:   []string{"Minimal change preserves outdated core assumptions"},
+		Differentiator: "Revises acceptance of propositions (vs Bayesian which revises degrees)",
+	},
+	{
+		ID:             "paraconsistent",
+		Code:           "E5",
+		Tier:           TierAdvanced,
+		Name:           "Paraconsistent Reasoning",
+		Category:       CategoryChange,
+		ShortDesc:      "Reason despite contradictions without explosion",

+// CatalogVersion is the version string for the embedded mode catalog.
+const CatalogVersion = "1.0.0"
+
+// DefaultCatalog returns the embedded mode catalog initialized and validated.
+func DefaultCatalog() (*ModeCatalog, error) {
